what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG line icon depicting a door with a rectangular panel, round knob, and baseline.
observations: Sets fill="none" on the root SVG while using currentColor for stroke.
Draws the door as a rounded rectangle from x=5 y=3 to width=14 height=18 with rx=1.
Draws the knob as a small filled circle at cx=15 cy=12 with radius .8.
Adds a horizontal base line from x=3 to x=21 at y=21.
Uses stroke width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
standouts: The icon is color-inheriting through currentColor.
The knob is filled while the rest of the icon is stroke-based.
The geometry is compact and centered within a 24x24 icon grid.
conventions: Follows common outline icon conventions with a 24x24 viewBox, currentColor stroke, rounded caps, and rounded joins.
Uses simple primitive SVG elements rather than paths for all visible shapes except the baseline.
Keeps the SVG self-contained and inline-compatible.
concerns: No title, description, role, or aria attributes are present for standalone accessibility semantics.
notes: A reusable monochrome outline door icon composed from basic SVG primitives.
questions: Is this icon intended to be decorative or exposed with semantic labeling when rendered?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a door icon, and the SVG renders a door-like outline with knob and baseline.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable door glyph that adapts to surrounding text or icon color via currentColor.
novelty: The filled currentColor knob contrasts with the otherwise stroke-only construction.
contribution: Contributes a compact door or entry/exit glyph with simple geometry, currentColor theming, and 24x24 outline icon styling for reuse in a fused design system.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a standalone SVG icon that draws a door using a rounded rectangle, filled circular knob, and baseline on a 24x24 currentColor outline grid.
intention_for_embedding: It was for representing a door-related UI concept as a reusable monochrome design-system icon.
